items.title: Spatiotemporal dynamics of tau extent and load increase in Alzheimer’s disease across four longitudinal cohorts
items.author: Pedro Rosa-Neto
items.content: Macedo et al. report the longitudinal spatiotemporal dynamics of tau from positron emission tomography. They find distinct dynamics in tau extent and load across Alzheimer’s disease stages, highlighting spatial extent metrics as sensitive indicators for early pathology, while load metrics correlate with advanced disease severity.

items.title: Spatial mapping and senolytic targeting of senescent and disease-associated microglia in aged mouse brain white matter
items.author: Marissa J. Schafer
items.content: Carver and colleagues profile the aged mouse brain using spatial transcriptomic and imaging analysis and identify a senescence- and disease-associated microglial-linked state enriched in white matter and reduced by senotherapeutic interventions.

items.title: Mapping and manipulating aged and senescent microglia
items.content: Single-cell spatial mapping and orthogonal approaches reveal a senescent and disease-associated microglial state that accumulates in the white matter of the aged mouse brain and is suppressed by senotherapeutic interventions.

items.title: Author Correction: A T<sub>reg</sub>-specific long noncoding RNA maintains immune-metabolic homeostasis in aging liver
items.author: Hua-Bing Li
items.content: Author Correction: A T-specific long noncoding RNA maintains immune-metabolic homeostasis in aging liver

items.title: Delayed molecular aging, preservation of energy metabolism and enhanced exercise response in exercise-trained human muscle
items.author: Riekelt H. Houtkooper
items.content: Exercise has an important role in healthy aging, yet the underlying molecular mechanisms are incompletely understood. Here the authors consider age, physical function and acute exercise in the context of the multiomics of human skeletal muscle, linking fitness to distinct aging profiles. Physically trained older individuals show ~50% fewer age-related differences and stronger exercise responses, and maintain expression of key energy metabolism and cellular respiration genes.

items.title: Therapeutic targeting of the conserved region within the low-complexity domain of TDP-43 is neuroprotective and extends survival in amyotrophic lateral sclerosis mice
items.author: Xinglong Wang
items.content: Gao et al. identify a toxicity-driving region within TDP-43 and show that a brain-penetrant small molecule targeting this region improves mitochondrial function, reduces neurodegeneration and extends survival in ALS mouse models.

items.title: Age-related molecular changes that are exercise independent
items.content: Multiomics analysis of human muscle suggests that physical fitness reshapes age-related molecular changes, and highlights distinct transcriptomic patterns of ‘preventable’ and ‘unavoidable’ alterations. Trained older adults retain youthful profiles, and stronger stress responses to exercise are associated with better health.

items.title: Targeting interleukin-11 to slow ovarian aging
items.author: Stuart A. Cook
items.content: As ovaries age, the ovarian stroma — the connective tissue in which follicles are embedded — becomes fibrotic and stiff, which leads to ovarian failure. Wu et al. show that ovarian expression of interleukin-11 (IL-11), which increases with age, promotes ovarian stiffening, which impairs reproductive function. In aged mice, inhibition of IL-11 improves ovarian function, restores hormone imbalances and promotes fertility.
